mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns as markets price in improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods when INDPRO accelerates, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a relatively low federal funds rate compresses real yields and forces expectations of aggressive future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for term premium movements.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals robust economic activity and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative forward returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surpassing 2.5% confirms strong aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors adjust valuation multiples upward.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of triggered quarters, or the predictive power disappears after controlling for prior market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and declines in the unemployment rate indicate a synchronized supply-labor expansion, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns through improved capacity utilization and earnings revisions.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.5%.
